steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "‘cinch' (a ______ comes from cincho." An opening parenthesis appears after "cinch," so the blank must supply the definition of cinch AND close that parenthesis before the sentence continues. Whatever punctuation follows the word "belt" must close the parenthetical.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "belt;": reconstructed: cinch (a belt; comes from cincho — the parenthesis is never closed, and a semicolon inside an unclosed parenthetical is a structural error. ✗
  • Choice B — "belt)": reconstructed: cinch (a belt) comes from cincho — the parenthesis opens, the definition "a belt" appears, the parenthesis closes, and the main sentence continues cleanly. ✓
  • Choice C — "belt—": reconstructed: cinch (a belt— comes from cincho — the parenthesis is never closed; an em dash inside an unclosed parenthetical creates an unresolvable structure. ✗
  • Choice D — "belt,": reconstructed: cinch (a belt, comes from cincho — the parenthesis is never closed; the comma only adds a pause inside the unclosed parenthetical. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
